chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Next we are going to cover two very related and important concepts of NumPy which are vectorization and broadcasting.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: We have already seen vectorization.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: We know that in Python, if we want to do any operation on a list, we have to use loops.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Loops perform operations element wise. That is why loops are slow and that is why operations on Python lists are slow.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: But in NumPy arrays, there is a concept of vectorization which means that our operations are performed as a whole on the entire array.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: We have seen many examples of this.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Let's look at one more example.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Let's do vectorization.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: In vectorization, first of all, we will create an array.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Let's create a NumPy 1D array.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: In which we will store some numbers.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Now let's suppose that we are going to perform a simple operation on this array.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Now when we perform an operation on NumPy arrays, we know that we can perform those operations directly.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: For example, if we want to take the square of this entire array.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: So I can simply do array multiplied to the power 2.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: And in this way, we will get the square of the entire array.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: But if we want to perform this operation on a normal simple Python list, then we have to use a loop.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: So this operation is possible in NumPy because of vectorization which is faster as compared to Python lists.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: In this way, we can perform more operations.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Let's suppose if I want to add 10 or maybe 5 to every number.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: In that case, this will automatically get added.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: If we want to add two arrays.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: For example, we have this array.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: And this is my array 2.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: And in array 2, we have numbers 6, 7, 8, 9 and 10.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: And I want to print my array plus array 2 values.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: So that is also very simple with the help of NumPy.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: So we have the sum of all the numbers of the array.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: 1 is added with 6.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: 2 is added with 7.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: 3 is added with 8.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: And in this way, we have vectorization working behind the scenes.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Now, just like we have vectorization as an important concept, there is another important concept which is called broadcasting.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: If we understand the meaning of broadcasting in detail in NumPy, it basically means scaling our arrays without using extra memory.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Basically, whenever we perform operations on arrays,

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: For example, we have values 1, 2, 3, 4 and 5.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Let's suppose we want to add value 10 or value 5 to all of these numbers.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Now whenever we are trying to add this value to this value in NumPy or in fact in Python,

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Then there is a mismatch in size.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: And generally, whenever we talk about broadcasting,

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Basically, in the context of mathematical operations, whenever there is a difference in the size of the NumPy arrays,

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: For example, if we look at this NumPy array, it has 5 elements.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Whereas, if we convert this 5 into an array, then there is only a single element in it.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: So how does NumPy arrange things so that this plus mathematical operation can be performed?

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: So there is the concept of broadcasting used.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Now in NumPy terms, we can call this array a vector.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: And this value is called a scalar value.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: If we have studied physics in school, then we must have heard these two names.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: The concept is similar.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Our scalars are basically independent values.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: We can call any number a scalar.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: And we can call this NumPy array a vector.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: So whenever we are performing a mathematical operation,

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: If we have a scalar with which we have to perform an operation of a vector,

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Then we can use broadcasting there.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: That is one use case of broadcasting.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: So basically, is broadcasting exactly?

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: We have talked so much, but what does it exactly mean?

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Broadcasting can be thought of in such a way that it scales our array.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Scaling means it can replicate things.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: For example, here only 5 is available.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: But if we convert this 5 into this particular form,

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Then we can perform the operation of these two vectors and arrays.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: So in that case, the size of this will be equivalent to this size.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: So in a way, whenever we have to perform a mathematical operation on two values,

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Where their size is incompatible,

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Now mathematical operations cannot be performed on them because the size is not matching.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: So we stretch the value of our small size.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: And stretching means that we make it compatible with the larger size value,

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Larger size array, and after that the operation of both of them can be done.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: The whole process of stretching and making it compatible,

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: There is no extra memory used in this process.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: We are talking here only at the conceptual level that the size will be equal in this way.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: But in actuality, the numbers are not repeated in the memory.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: So we can read about the internal implementation of NumPy in the documentation.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: But this is the general concept which takes place.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: That our incompatible sizes where there is a smaller size array,

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: It is stretched so that the size of the large size array is equivalent to it.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: And then our operation can be performed.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: And broadcasting cannot be used to perform operations on every possible size array.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: For that there are some rules.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: What are the rules? We can go to the official documentation of NumPy.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: And the rules of general broadcasting say that when operating on two different arrays,

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Here we took the example of a scalar, but there could have been an array here too.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: NumPy compares their shape element wise.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: It starts with the trailing, trailing means the rightmost value.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Trailing means at the end.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: So if there is 3x2 in the shape, then it will first compare 2 and then 3.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: So there are comparisons of the dimensions right to left.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Two dimensions are compatible when they are equal or one of them is 1.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: For example, if we have an array whose shape is 3x2 and an array whose shape is 5x2.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Broadcasting cannot be performed on both of them.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Because 2 and 2 match but 5 and 3 do not match.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: But if we had a 1 in the shape instead of 5, then broadcasting can be performed on these two arrays.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Because 2 matches with 2 and 3 does not match with 1 but because this is 1.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Then that makes the size or the shape compatible for broadcasting.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: So for broadcasting, one of these two conditions should be met for each single dimension.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Let's look at some examples of broadcasting.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Let's suppose we take the example of the same array here.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Let's suppose we want to perform an operation on this array.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: We want to add 5 values in the array.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: So in that case, we successfully added 5 and broadcasting is used here behind the scenes.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: If we create an array 2, let's bring this array 1 here.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: This is our array 1 and in the same way we will create an array 2.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: But in the size of array 2, we are going to add 5 values.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: In this way, we have 2D array and what we want is to print the sum of array and array 2.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: So we have our output in this way.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Now in this output, we will notice that these values have been added with these values and these values have been added with these values.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: And if we print the shape of these arrays, then we will get the output of array 2.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: So we will get the output of array 2.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Now let's look at the output of array 2.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: So, the output of this array is we get 5 values and we are going to print the shape of this array.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: And if we print the shape of these array then we will get the output of array 2.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Similarly, we can also print the shape of array 2.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Now when we are saying 5 here, we are saying that this array has a shape of 5 comma printed here.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: But can we say that this shape is basically 1 x 5?

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Because there is one row and five columns.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: Similarly, there is a second array which is of 5.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: and 5 columns. Similarly, the shape of the second array is 2 x 5.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: So, the dimension of 5 and 5 is compatible.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: These two are not matching, they are not the same values. But because here is one dimension,

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: they become compatible. If there was no 1 here, there would be something else.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: For example, in the same array,

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: if we had 5 values here,

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: then we would get an error. Here we got an error that

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: the reference could not be broadcast together with shapes.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: 5 is matching with 5 but 3 is not matching with 2.

chunks.title: Vectorization
chunks.text: So, for them, broadcasting is not possible.

text: Next we are going to cover two very related and important concepts of NumPy which are vectorization and broadcasting. We have already seen vectorization. We know that in Python, if we want to do any operation on a list, we have to use loops. Loops perform operations element wise. That is why loops are slow and that is why operations on Python lists are slow. But in NumPy arrays, there is a concept of vectorization which means that our operations are performed as a whole on the entire array. We have seen many examples of this. Let's look at one more example. Let's do vectorization. In vectorization, first of all, we will create an array. Let's create a NumPy 1D array. In which we will store some numbers. 1, 2, 3, 4 and 5. Now let's suppose that we are going to perform a simple operation on this array. Now when we perform an operation on NumPy arrays, we know that we can perform those operations directly. For example, if we want to take the square of this entire array. So I can simply do array multiplied to the power 2. And in this way, we will get the square of the entire array. But if we want to perform this operation on a normal simple Python list, then we have to use a loop. So this operation is possible in NumPy because of vectorization which is faster as compared to Python lists. In this way, we can perform more operations. Let's suppose if I want to add 10 or maybe 5 to every number. In that case, this will automatically get added. If we want to add two arrays. For example, we have this array. And this is my array 2. And in array 2, we have numbers 6, 7, 8, 9 and 10. And I want to print my array plus array 2 values. So that is also very simple with the help of NumPy. So we have the sum of all the numbers of the array. 1 is added with 6. 2 is added with 7. 3 is added with 8. And in this way, we have vectorization working behind the scenes. Now, just like we have vectorization as an important concept, there is another important concept which is called broadcasting. If we understand the meaning of broadcasting in detail in NumPy, it basically means scaling our arrays without using extra memory. Basically, whenever we perform operations on arrays, For example, we have values 1, 2, 3, 4 and 5. Let's suppose we want to add value 10 or value 5 to all of these numbers. Now whenever we are trying to add this value to this value in NumPy or in fact in Python, Then there is a mismatch in size. And generally, whenever we talk about broadcasting, Basically, in the context of mathematical operations, whenever there is a difference in the size of the NumPy arrays, For example, if we look at this NumPy array, it has 5 elements. Whereas, if we convert this 5 into an array, then there is only a single element in it. So how does NumPy arrange things so that this plus mathematical operation can be performed? So there is the concept of broadcasting used. Now in NumPy terms, we can call this array a vector. And this value is called a scalar value. If we have studied physics in school, then we must have heard these two names. The concept is similar. Our scalars are basically independent values. We can call any number a scalar. And we can call this NumPy array a vector. So whenever we are performing a mathematical operation, If we have a scalar with which we have to perform an operation of a vector, Then we can use broadcasting there. That is one use case of broadcasting. So basically, is broadcasting exactly? We have talked so much, but what does it exactly mean? Broadcasting can be thought of in such a way that it scales our array. Scaling means it can replicate things. For example, here only 5 is available. But if we convert this 5 into this particular form, Then we can perform the operation of these two vectors and arrays. So in that case, the size of this will be equivalent to this size. So in a way, whenever we have to perform a mathematical operation on two values, Where their size is incompatible, Now mathematical operations cannot be performed on them because the size is not matching. So we stretch the value of our small size. And stretching means that we make it compatible with the larger size value, Larger size array, and after that the operation of both of them can be done. The whole process of stretching and making it compatible, There is no extra memory used in this process. We are talking here only at the conceptual level that the size will be equal in this way. But in actuality, the numbers are not repeated in the memory. So we can read about the internal implementation of NumPy in the documentation. But this is the general concept which takes place. That our incompatible sizes where there is a smaller size array, It is stretched so that the size of the large size array is equivalent to it. And then our operation can be performed. And broadcasting cannot be used to perform operations on every possible size array. For that there are some rules. What are the rules? We can go to the official documentation of NumPy. And the rules of general broadcasting say that when operating on two different arrays, Here we took the example of a scalar, but there could have been an array here too. NumPy compares their shape element wise. It starts with the trailing, trailing means the rightmost value. Trailing means at the end. So if there is 3x2 in the shape, then it will first compare 2 and then 3. So there are comparisons of the dimensions right to left. Two dimensions are compatible when they are equal or one of them is 1. For example, if we have an array whose shape is 3x2 and an array whose shape is 5x2. Broadcasting cannot be performed on both of them. Because 2 and 2 match but 5 and 3 do not match. But if we had a 1 in the shape instead of 5, then broadcasting can be performed on these two arrays. Because 2 matches with 2 and 3 does not match with 1 but because this is 1. Then that makes the size or the shape compatible for broadcasting. So for broadcasting, one of these two conditions should be met for each single dimension. Let's look at some examples of broadcasting. Let's suppose we take the example of the same array here. Let's suppose we want to perform an operation on this array. We want to add 5 values in the array. So in that case, we successfully added 5 and broadcasting is used here behind the scenes. If we create an array 2, let's bring this array 1 here. This is our array 1 and in the same way we will create an array 2. But in the size of array 2, we are going to add 5 values. In this way, we have 2D array and what we want is to print the sum of array and array 2. So we have our output in this way. Now in this output, we will notice that these values have been added with these values and these values have been added with these values. And if we print the shape of these arrays, then we will get the output of array 2. So we will get the output of array 2. Now let's look at the output of array 2. So, the output of this array is we get 5 values and we are going to print the shape of this array. And if we print the shape of these array then we will get the output of array 2. Similarly, we can also print the shape of array 2. Now when we are saying 5 here, we are saying that this array has a shape of 5 comma printed here. But can we say that this shape is basically 1 x 5? Because there is one row and five columns. Similarly, there is a second array which is of 5. and 5 columns. Similarly, the shape of the second array is 2 x 5. So, the dimension of 5 and 5 is compatible. These two are not matching, they are not the same values. But because here is one dimension, they become compatible. If there was no 1 here, there would be something else. For example, in the same array, if we had 5 values here, then we would get an error. Here we got an error that the reference could not be broadcast together with shapes. 5 is matching with 5 but 3 is not matching with 2. So, for them, broadcasting is not possible.
